hawkish_phrases: Further tightening in monetary policy would be required if there were evidence of more persistent inflationary pressures
Key indicators of UK inflation persistence remained elevated
Three members preferred to increase Bank Rate by 0.25 percentage points, to 5.5%
Monetary policy will need to be sufficiently restrictive for sufficiently long
There remained upside risks to the outlook for wage growth
dovish_phrases: Annual private sector regular Average Weekly Earnings (AWE) growth declined to 7.3%
For one member, the risks of overtightening policy had continued to build
Twelve-month CPI inflation fell sharply from 6.7% in September to 4.6% in October
some loosening in the labour market
summary: The MPC voted 6-3 to hold Bank Rate at 5.25% with three members dissenting in favor of a 25bp hike to 5.5%, emphasizing that policy must remain sufficiently restrictive for sufficiently long and that further tightening would be required if more persistent inflationary pressures emerged.
